During the time that; at the time of the action of the following clause or participle phrase.
examples: A player wins when [as soon as, or at any time that, if] she has four cards of the same suit.
Be careful when crossing the street.

definitions: That's enough, a command to stop adding something, especially an ingredient of food or drink.
Expressing impatience. (Compare what.)
The place in which something happens.
examples: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

definitions: Interrogative adverb, used in either a direct or indirect question: at what place; to what place; what place.
In what situation.
examples: He asked where I grew up.
Where would we be without our parents?

definitions: The place in which.
examples: He lives within five miles of where he was born.

definitions: While on the contrary; although; whereas.
At or in which place or situation.
examples: Where Susy has trouble coloring inside the lines, Johnny has already mastered shading.
He is looking for a house where he can have a complete office.

definitions: Which of two.
Introducing a direct interrogative question (often with correlative or) which indicates doubt between alternatives.
Used to introduce an indirect interrogative question that consists of multiple alternative possibilities (usually with correlative or).
examples: He chose the correct answer, but I don't know whether it was by luck or (whether it was) by skill.
Do you know whether he's coming?

definitions: (interrogative) What one or ones (of those mentioned or implied).
The/Any ones that; whichever.
examples: By now, you must surely know which is which.
Please take which you please.

definitions: An uncertain duration of time, a period of time.
examples: He lectured for quite a long while.

definitions: To pass (time) idly.
To occupy or entertain (someone) in order to let time pass.
Until.
During the same time that.
Although.
examples: He was sleeping while I was singing.
This case, while interesting, is a bit frustrating.

definitions: The color/colour of snow or milk; the colour of light containing equal amounts of all visible wavelengths.
A person of European descent with light-coloured skin.
To make white; to whiten; to bleach.
Bright and colourless; reflecting equal quantities of all frequencies of visible light.
(sometimes capitalized) Of or relating to Caucasians, people of European descent with light-coloured skin.
examples: Write in black ink on white paper.
white drinking fountain

definitions: A person under discussion; a question of which person.
(interrogative) What person or people; which person or people; asks for the identity of someone. (used in a direct or indirect question)
(interrogative) What is one's position; asks whether someone deserves to say or do something.
examples: I don't know who it is. (indirect question)
I don't like what you did, but who am I to criticize you? I've done worse.

definitions: Something complete, without any parts missing.
An entirety.
examples: This variety of fascinating details didn't fall together into an enjoyable, coherent whole.

definitions: Entire, undivided.
Sound, uninjured, healthy.
examples: I ate a whole fish.
He is of whole mind, but the same cannot be said about his physical state.

definitions: In entirety; entirely; wholly.
examples: I ate a fish whole!

definitions: (interrogative) What person or people; which person or people.
(relative) Used to refer to a previously mentioned person or people.
examples: He's a person with whom I work. (defining)
To whom it may concern, all business of John Smith Ltd. has now been transferred to Floggitt & Runne.

definitions: (interrogative) That or those of whom or belonging to whom.
(relative) That or those of whom or belonging to whom.
examples: Several people have lost their suitcases. Whose have you found?
This car is blocking the way, but Mr Smith, whose it is, will be here shortly.

definitions: Reason
A young heifer.
examples: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

definitions: For what cause, reason, or purpose (interrogative adverb).
For which cause, reason, or purpose (relative adverb).
examples: That's the reason why I did that.

definitions: An exclamation used to express indignation, mild surprise, or impatience.
A ball that passes so far from the batsman that the umpire deems it unplayable; the arm signal used by an umpire to signal a wide; the extra run added to the batting side's score
Having a large physical extent from side to side.
Large in scope.
examples: We walked down a wide corridor.
The inquiry had a wide remit.

definitions: Extensively
Completely
examples: He travelled far and wide.
He was wide awake.

definitions: A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.
The female of a pair of mated animals.
examples: A new wife for the gander is introduced into the pen.

definitions: (said of men) to marry
(now uncommon or literary) To wish, desire (something).
(nowadays rare) To wish or desire (that something happen); to intend (that).
examples: Do what you will.
I’ve told him three times, but he won’t take his medicine.

definitions: One's independent faculty of choice; the ability to be able to exercise one's choice or intention.
One's intention or decision; someone's orders or commands.
examples: Of course, man's will is often regulated by his reason.
Eventually I submitted to my parents' will.

definitions: To conquer, defeat.
To reach some destination or object, despite difficulty or toil (now usually intransitive, with preposition or locative adverb).
examples: to win the jackpot in a lottery;  to win a bottle of wine in a raffle
Who would win in a fight between an octopus and a dolphin?

definitions: An individual victory.
A feat carried out successfully; a victorious achievement.
examples: Our first win of the season put us in high spirits.

definitions: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
Air artificially put in motion by any force or action.
examples: As they accelerated onto the motorway, the wind tore the plywood off the car's roof-rack.
the wind of a cannon ball;  the wind of a bellows

definitions: To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.
To cause (someone) to become breathless, as by a blow to the abdomen, or by physical exertion, running, etc.
examples: The boxer was winded during round two.
The hounds winded the game.

definitions: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
Air artificially put in motion by any force or action.
examples: As they accelerated onto the motorway, the wind tore the plywood off the car's roof-rack.
the wind of a cannon ball;  the wind of a bellows

definitions: To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.
To cause (someone) to become breathless, as by a blow to the abdomen, or by physical exertion, running, etc.
examples: The boxer was winded during round two.
The hounds winded the game.

definitions: The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant.
Straw or hay cut up fine for the food of cattle.
examples: To separate out the chaff, early cultures tossed baskets of grain into the air and let the wind blow away the lighter chaff.
launch window;  window of opportunity;  You have a two-hour window of clear weather to finish working on the lawn.

definitions: To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting the juice of grapes.
An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting the juice of fruits or vegetables other than grapes, usually preceded by the type of the fruit or vegetable; for example, "dandelion wine".
examples: She ordered some wine for the meal.
I'd like three beers and two wines, please.

definitions: To entertain with wine.
To drink wine.

Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
examples: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions: A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.
An expression of such a desire, often connected with ideas of magic and supernatural power.
examples: My dearest wish is to see them happily married.

definitions: To desire; to want.
To hope (+ object clause with may or in present subjunctive).
examples: I'll come tomorrow, if you wish it.
We wish you a Merry Christmas.

definitions: Along, together with others, in a group, etc.
examples: Do you want to come with?

definitions: Against.
In the company of; alongside, close to; near to.
examples: He picked a fight with the class bully.
He went with his friends.

definitions: A flexible, slender twig or shoot, especially when used as a band or for binding; a withy.
A band of twisted twigs.
An adult female human.
(collective) All females collectively; womankind.
To staff with female labor.
To make effeminate or womanish.
The substance making up the central part of the trunk and branches of a tree. Used as a material for construction, to manufacture various items, etc. or as fuel.
The wood of a particular species of tree.
examples: There was lots of wood on the beach.
Teak is much used for outdoor benches, but a number of other woods are also suitable, such as ipé, redwood, etc.

definitions: To cover or plant with trees.
To hide behind trees.
examples: to wood a steamboat or a locomotive

definitions: Mad, insane, crazed.
The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)
Something like such a unit of language:
examples: mum's the word
Have you had any word from John yet?

definitions: To say or write (something) using particular words; to phrase (something).
To flatter with words, to cajole.
examples: I’m not sure how to word this letter to the council.
Well worth thee, me friend.

definitions: Truth, indeed, that is the truth! The shortened form of the statement "My word is my bond."
(stereotypically) An abbreviated form of word up; a statement of the acknowledgment of fact with a hint of nonchalant approval.
examples: "Yo, that movie was epic!" / "Word?" ("You speak the truth?") / "Word." ("I speak the truth.")

definitions: (heading) Employment.
(heading) Effort.
examples: We don't have much time. Let's get to work piling up those sandbags.
Tell me you're using clean works at least.

definitions: To do a specific task by employing physical or mental powers.
To effect by gradual degrees.
examples: He’s working in a bar.
he worked his way through the crowd

definitions: A person who performs labor for a living, especially manual labor.
A nonreproductive social insect, such as ant, bee, termite, or wasp.
examples: This FTP client spawns a separate worker for each file to be uploaded.

definitions: (with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.
The Universe.
examples: There will always be lovers, till the world’s end.
People are dying of starvation all over the world.

definitions: To consider or cause to be considered from a global perspective; to consider as a global whole, rather than making or focussing on national or other distinctions; compare globalise.
To make real; to make worldly.
A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
More loosely, any of various tubular invertebrates resembling annelids but not closely related to them, such as velvet worms, acorn worms, flatworms, or roundworms.
examples: Don't try to run away, you little worm!

definitions: To make (one's way) with a crawling motion.
To move with one's body dragging the ground.
examples: We wormed our way through the underbrush.
He wormed his way into the organization

definitions: A strong feeling of anxiety.
An instance or cause of such a feeling.
examples: I'm afflicted by worry throughout the night.
My main worry is that I'll miss the train.

definitions: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
Disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress.
examples: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.
Your tone of voice worries me.

definitions: Unfavorable; negative; not good.
Not suitable or fitting.
examples: Abducting children is bad for mothers.
Do you think it is a bad idea to confront him directly?

definitions: In a bad manner.
Very much; to a great degree.
examples: I want it so badly.

definitions: Loss; disadvantage; defeat.
That which is worse; something less good.
examples: Do not think the worse of him for his enterprise.

definitions: To make worse; to put at disadvantage; to discomfit.
Unfavorable; negative; not good.
Not suitable or fitting.
examples: Abducting children is bad for mothers.
Do you think it is a bad idea to confront him directly?

definitions: Something or someone that is the worst
To make worse.
To grow worse; to deteriorate.
Superlative of bad
Superlative of ill
examples: My sore leg hurts worst when it's cold and rainy.

definitions: Something that is immoral or not good.
An instance of wronging someone (sometimes with possessive to indicate the wrongdoer).
examples: Injustice is a heinous wrong.

definitions: To treat unjustly; to injure or harm.
To deprive of some right, or to withhold some act of justice.
Incorrect or untrue.
Asserting something incorrect or untrue.
examples: Some of your answers were correct, and some were wrong.
You're wrong: he's not Superman at all.

definitions: In a way that isn't right; incorrectly, wrongly.
examples: I spelled several names wrong in my address book.

definitions: A solar year, the time it takes the Earth to complete one revolution of the Sun (between 365.24 and 365.26 days depending on the point of reference).

(by extension) The time it takes for any astronomical object (such as a planet, dwarf planet, small Solar System body, or comet) in direct orbit around a star (such as the Sun) to make one revolution around the star.
examples: we moved to this town a year ago;  I quit smoking exactly one year ago
Mars goes around the sun once in a Martian year, or 1.88 Earth years.

definitions: The day immediately before today; one day ago.
The recent past, often disparaging.
examples: Today is the child of yesterday and the parent of tomorrow.
yesterday's technology

definitions: On the day before today.
As soon as possible.
examples: I started to watch the video yesterday, but could only finish it this evening.
I want this done yesterday!

definitions: (usually with negative) Thus far; up to the present; up to some specified time; still
Continuously up to the current time; still.
examples: He has never yet been late for an appointment;   I’m not yet wise enough to answer that;   Have you finished yet?
The workers went to the factory early and are striking yet.

definitions: Nevertheless; however; but; despite that.
examples: I thought I knew you, yet how wrong I was.

definitions: A metal pan or boiler; yetling.
To melt; found; cast, as metal.
To get.
To address (a person) using the pronoun you (in the past, especially to use you rather than thou, when you was considered more formal).
(object pronoun) The people spoken, or written to, as an object.
(reflexive pronoun) (To) yourselves, (to) yourself.
examples: Both of you should get ready now.
You are all supposed to do as I tell you.

definitions: People who are young; young people, collectively; youth.
Young or immature offspring (especially of an animal).
examples: The young of today are well-educated.
The lion caught a gnu to feed its young.

definitions: To become or seem to become younger.
To cause to appear younger.
In the early part of growth or life; born not long ago.
At an early stage of existence or development; having recently come into existence.
examples: a lamb is a young sheep;  these picture books are for young readers
the age of space travel is still young;   a young business

definitions: Of something highly offensive; causing aversion or disgust.
(lighthearted) Delicious.
A mother who is sexually attractive.
The numeric symbol that represents the cardinal number zero.
The digit 0 in the decimal, binary, and all other base numbering systems.
examples: In unary and k-adic notation in general, zero is the empty string.
One million has six zeroes.

definitions: To set a measuring instrument to zero; to calibrate instrument scale to valid zero.
To change a memory location or range to values of zero; to set a variable in a computer program to zero.
examples: Zero the fluorometer with the same solvent used in extraction.
Results were inconsistent because an array wasn’t zeroed during initialization.

definitions: No, not any
Of a cloud ceiling, limiting vision to 50 feet (15 meters) or less.
examples: She showed zero respect.
The stem of "kobieta" with the zero ending is "kobiet".

definitions: The cardinal number occurring before one and that denotes no quantity or amount at all, represented in Arabic numerals as 0.
examples: A cheque for zero dollars and zero cents crashed the computers on division by zero.

definitions: A park where live animals are exhibited.
Any place that is wild, crowded, or chaotic.
examples: The shopping center was a zoo the week before Christmas.
